back_translation: Brethren, we want you to know about the grace of God which has been given to the churches in the province of Macedonia.
Namely, in the heavy testing through affliction, their overflowing joy and their deep poverty also overflowed into rich generosity.
For I testify that they offered according to their strength and even beyond strength, voluntarily.
They pleaded with us with great urgency, that they might receive the grace and the participation in the service to the saints.
And not as we had expected, but they offered themselves first to the Lord, and then to us, according to the will of God.
Therefore, we asked Titus that, just as he had begun, he should also bring this grace to completion in you.
But just as you overflow in everything — in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in all earnestness, and in the love of ours that is in you — let you also overflow in this grace.
I do not say this as a command, but I speak in order to test the genuineness of your love through the earnestness of others.
For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that even though he was rich, he consented to become poor for you, so that you might become rich through his poverty.
I give my opinion on this matter, because this is beneficial for you, who not only began to act, but also were the first to have intent since last year.
Now, let you also bring it to completion, so that the intent to act will match with the completion according to the strength you have.
For if intent is already present, the offering is acceptable according to what each person has, not according to what he does not have.
For it is not so that others should be at ease and you should be in difficulty, but so that there should be equality.
At this present time, your overflow will help in their lack, so that their overflow will also help in your lack on a future occasion, so that there will be equality.
Just as it is written, 'The one who gathered much had no excess, and the one who gathered little had no lack.'
Thanks be to God, who has placed the same earnestness for you in the heart of Titus.
For he received our request, and moreover, since he was even more earnest, he set out toward you voluntarily.
And we sent a certain brother along with him, who has been praised in the matter of the gospel throughout all the churches.
And not only that, but he was also appointed by the churches to be our travel-companion in the service of this grace, for the glory of the Lord himself, and to demonstrate our intent.
We are taking precaution so that no one would criticize us in the matter of this generous offering which we manage.
For we take care to do what is good, not only before the face of the Lord, but also before the eyes of people.
And we sent a brother of ours along with them, whom we have proven in many matters and many times to be earnest, and now even more earnest, because he has great confidence in you.
If concerning Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker in proclaiming among you. If concerning our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, and they are the glory of Christ.
Therefore, let you demonstrate the proof of your love, and the cause of our pride in you, before them, in the presence of the churches.
